timeline.helperFallbackTip: The agent didn't explicitly send a reply; this message was written by the fallback LLM from its reasoning.

onboarding.heading: A fresh page.
onboarding.framing: Nothing here yet — and that's the point. The more you and {{name}} work together, the more it remembers, connects, and carries forward.
onboarding.prompt1: Summarise this week's most important updates for me
onboarding.prompt2: Connect a channel and watch it for anything urgent
onboarding.prompt3: Set up a Monday-morning digest of what matters

team.emptyHint: @mention a teammate to start the conversation — they hand off to each other from there.
team.noAgents: No agents in this team yet.
team.placeholder: Message the team — @mention to address someone…

inner.empty: No execution steps for this turn

error.situationFailed: This run hit an error and the agent could not reply normally. Details:
error.situationRecovered: The agent replied, but errors occurred during the run. Details:

discord.errOwnerIdNumeric: Owner user ID must be numeric (Discord → Settings → Advanced → Developer Mode, then right-click your name → Copy User ID).
discord.unbindConfirmMessage: The agent will stop receiving Discord messages and lose all Discord tools until you bind again.
discord.intro: Bind a Discord bot to chat with your agent in servers and DMs. You'll need a Bot Token from the Discord Developer Portal.
discord.setupQuestion: How do I get a Bot Token?
discord.step1: Open the <2><0></0></2>, click <4>New Application</4>, then open the <6>Bot</6> tab.
discord.step2: <0>REQUIRED:</0> on the Bot page, under <3>Privileged Gateway Intents</3>, turn ON <5>Message Content Intent</5>. Without it the bot receives messages with an empty body and can't read what people say.
discord.step3: Click <1>Reset Token</1> → <3>Copy</3>. Paste it below. Keep it secret.
discord.step4: Open <1>OAuth2 → URL Generator</1>, tick scope <3>bot</3> and permissions <5>View Channels</5>, <7>Send Messages</7>, <9>Read Message History</9>; open the generated URL and invite the bot to your server.
discord.step5: Optional: paste <1>your</1> numeric Discord user ID below so the agent recognises owner-vs-stranger. Enable Discord → Settings → Advanced → <3>Developer Mode</3>, then right-click your name → <5>Copy User ID</5>.
discord.troubleBlank: <0>Bot sees blank messages?</0> Message Content Intent is off — re-enable it on the Bot page.
discord.troubleNoReply: <0>No replies in a server?</0> @-mention the bot; it stays silent in channels until addressed.
discord.ownerIdPlaceholder: Your Discord user ID (optional, enables owner trust signal)
discord.noOwner: ⚠ No owner registered — agent has no trust signal for Discord senders. Re-bind with your numeric user ID to enable.

telegram.errTokenFormat: Bot token format looks wrong. Expected <digits>:<base64> (e.g. 7981632450:AAH-...).
telegram.unbindConfirmMessage: The agent will stop receiving Telegram messages and lose all Telegram tools until you bind again.
telegram.intro: Bind a Telegram bot to chat with your agent in DMs and groups. You'll need a Bot Token from @BotFather.
telegram.setupQuestion: How do I get a Bot Token?
telegram.step1: Open Telegram, search <2><0></0></2>, start a chat. Send <4>/newbot</4>; pick a display name + username (must end in <6>bot</6>).
telegram.step2: BotFather replies with a token like <1>7981632450:AAH-kxRP...</1>. Paste it below.
telegram.step3: <0>Privacy mode — KEEP DEFAULT ON</0>. By default the bot only sees <2>/commands</2> and @-mentions in groups; this is the right behavior for almost all use cases (saves tokens, prevents spam). DMs are unaffected. Do NOT run <4>/setprivacy → Disable</4> unless you specifically want the bot to see every group message (rare research / note-taking bots only).
telegram.step4: Optional: send <1>/setjoingroups</1> → bot → <3>Enable</3> if you want the bot to be addable to groups.
telegram.step5: Optional: paste <1>your</1> Telegram @username below so the agent recognises owner-vs-stranger DMs. <3>After binding, send any message to the bot from your @username</3> — Telegram's API only reveals your numeric user_id on a real DM, so the trust signal activates on first contact (not at bind time).
telegram.troubleNoReply: <0>Group not getting replies?</0> @-mention the bot. Privacy mode is intentionally on — flipping it would make the bot try to reply to everything in the group.
telegram.usernamePlaceholder: Your Telegram @username (optional, enables owner trust signal)
telegram.ownerPending: ⏳ Owner registration pending — DM @{{bot}} on Telegram from @{{username}} once to activate the trust signal.
telegram.ownerPendingNote: (Telegram's API doesn't let bots resolve @usernames at bind time — we wait for the first matching DM, then auto-link.)
telegram.noOwner: ⚠ No owner registered — agent has no trust signal for Telegram DMs. Re-bind with your @username to enable.

slack.errClipboard: Could not copy to clipboard. Select the YAML manually instead.
slack.unbindConfirmMessage: The agent will stop receiving Slack messages and lose all Slack tools until you bind again.
slack.intro: Bind a Slack bot to enable messaging in DMs and channels. You'll need a Bot Token (<1>xoxb-...</1>) and an App-Level Token (<3>xapp-...</3>).
slack.setupQuestion: How do I get these tokens?
slack.step1: Open <2><0></0></2>, click <4>Create New App</4> → <6>From an app manifest</6>, pick your workspace.
slack.step2: Paste the YAML below into the manifest editor. You can edit <2>display_information.name</2> (the app name in your workspace admin) and <5>features.bot_user.display_name</5> (the bot's name in DMs and @-mentions) — the defaults are placeholders. Leave the scopes / events / <7>socket_mode_enabled</7> bits as-is. Then click <10>Next</10> → <13>Create</13>. Slack will pre-configure ~16 OAuth scopes, 5 event subscriptions, Socket Mode, and the bot user.
slack.step3: On the app page, click <1>Install App</1> → <4>Install to Workspace</4> → <6>Allow</6>. Copy the <8>Bot User OAuth Token</8> (<10>xoxb-...</10>) into the field below.
slack.step4: Click <1>Basic Information</1> → scroll to <4>App-Level Tokens</4> → <6>Generate Token and Scopes</6>. Add the <8>connections:write</8> scope, click Generate, and copy the token (<10>xapp-...</10>) into the second field below.
slack.step5: After binding, invite the bot to any channel where it should listen: <1>/invite @NarraNexus</1>. DMs work automatically.
slack.emailPlaceholder: Your Slack email (optional, enables owner trust signal)
slack.noOwner: ⚠ No owner registered — agent has no trust signal for Slack DMs. Re-bind with your Slack email to enable.

wechat.errNoQr: Could not get a WeChat login QR
wechat.disconnectConfirmMessage: The agent will stop receiving WeChat messages and lose all WeChat tools until you scan in again.
wechat.intro: Connect your personal WeChat so the agent can chat with you in WeChat DMs. You'll scan a login QR with the WeChat app on your phone — no password or token needed.
wechat.warning: ⚠ This signs the agent in as a personal WeChat account via a third-party gateway. Use an account you control; personal-account automation is outside WeChat's official Bot terms.
wechat.scanInstruction: Open WeChat on your phone → <1>Discover → Scan</1>, and scan this code. Keep this panel open until it confirms.
wechat.cantScan: Can't scan? open the QR page
wechat.ownerPending: ⏳ Owner registration pending — send any message to this account from your own WeChat once to activate the owner trust signal.
wechat.ownerPendingNote: (The gateway only reveals your wxid on a real DM, so the signal activates on first contact, not at scan time.)

lark.unbindConfirmMessage: Unbind this Lark bot? This will remove all Lark inbox data for this agent.
lark.appIdValidation: App ID should start with 'cli_' followed by ≥8 alphanumeric characters. Copy it from the developer console's Credentials & Basic Info page.
lark.openConsole: Open the relevant developer console page
lark.intro: Bind a Feishu/Lark bot to enable messaging, contacts, docs, calendar, and tasks.
lark.completeOauth: Complete OAuth to unlock search features (contacts by name, messages, documents)
lark.mismatchTitle: Wrong platform selected — the bot will not receive messages.
lark.mismatchExplain: but the App ID is registered on the other platform. The WebSocket subscriber was rejected with error 1000040351.

agentList.deleteAgentMessage: Delete agent "{{name}}"? This will permanently remove all related data (narratives, events, instances, jobs, etc.).
agentList.deleteAgentError: Error deleting agent. Please try again.
agentList.deleteTeamMessage: Delete team "{{name}}"? Members are unlinked; the agents themselves are NOT deleted.

commandPalette.placeholder: Jump to an agent or page…

artifacts.removeNote: Only the registry rows are removed. Each agent's workspace files stay where they were written — clean those up from the workspace section of each agent's config panel if you want to free disk space.
artifacts.empty: You don't have any artifacts yet. They'll appear here as agents create them.

quota.uninitialized: System free-tier quota is not set up for your account yet. Please contact an administrator, or configure your own provider below to continue.
quota.title: System Free-Tier Quota
quota.preferToggle: Use free-tier quota even when I have my own provider configured
quota.preferOn: Currently: routing through system provider — free-tier usage applies. Falls back to your own provider if the free tier runs out.
quota.preferOff: Currently: using your own provider when configured; free tier only applies when you have no provider set.
quota.exhaustedPreferOn: Free tier used up — turn this off to route through your own provider now, or top up to keep using the free tier.
quota.exhaustedPreferOff: Free tier used up — currently using your own provider. This re-enables automatically once your quota is replenished.
quota.exhaustedNote: Free tier consumed. Add your own provider below, or uncheck the toggle above to route through your existing provider.

netmind.subtitle: Power plan & credits · used for your LLM API usage
netmind.error: Could not load your NetMind.AI Power account. If your login expired, sign in again and refresh.
netmind.expiresDowngrade: Valid until {{date}}, then downgrades to Free
netmind.cancelConfirm: Cancel = turn off auto-renew. You stay on Pro until the period ends — no immediate downgrade, no prorated refund. Continue?
netmind.reactivateConfirm: Resume auto-renew for your NetMind.AI Power Pro subscription?
netmind.awaitingPayment: Waiting for payment to complete… this panel refreshes automatically. If you already paid, come back to this tab.
netmind.pollTimeout: Still not active. If you completed payment, refresh in a moment.
netmind.notEligible: Cannot incur paid usage right now (no balance / not eligible).
netmind.useDesc: Run agent conversations on your NetMind.AI Power credits — no API key to paste.
netmind.useSubscribeOk: Connected — you can now pick a model in LLM Providers.
netmind.deductOrder: subscription grant first → then account balance (recharge) → paid usage stops when both run out.
netmind.scopeNote: These NetMind.AI Power credits cover LLM API usage. Compute (GPU) and other pricing are billed separately.
netmind.sandboxNotice: The NarraNexus sandbox itself is free for now (no sandbox-service charge); billing will start later, with notice.
netmind.rechargeDesc: Top up your NetMind.AI Power balance. Credits are kept regardless of plan.
netmind.rechargeProcessing: Waiting for payment… complete it in the opened window; your balance updates automatically.

modal.title: Settings
modal.slotAgentDesc: The "brain" of your AI agent. This model handles all conversations with users, makes decisions, and executes tasks. A more capable model here means smarter, more nuanced responses.
modal.slotHelperDesc: A secondary AI model used for behind-the-scenes analysis: summarizing conversations, extracting key information, and generating internal reports. Does not talk to users directly, but influences the quality of the agent's background processing.
modal.slotsIntro: NarraNexus uses two AI model slots for different purposes. You can use the same provider for both, or mix and match to optimize for cost, speed, or quality.
modal.privacyIntro: Control what data NarraNexus collects to improve the product.
modal.analyticsDesc: Allow NarraNexus to collect anonymous usage data to improve the product. No conversation content is ever collected.

provider.presetNetmindDesc: One key covers both Anthropic & OpenAI endpoints
provider.pendingHint: Press Enter or click + to add “{{model}}” — otherwise it will be discarded.
provider.suggestionsHint: Suggestions — click to add. You can still type custom model names above.
provider.syncNothing: All providers already have the latest models — nothing to add.
provider.auxModelWarning: All auxiliary tasks will use this model. May affect speed/cost.
provider.noProtocolProvider: No {{protocol}} protocol provider configured. Add one in Step 1 above.
provider.section1Subtitle: Add API keys for your LLM providers. Each preset creates both Anthropic and OpenAI protocol endpoints automatically.
provider.quickAddDesc: Select a provider, paste your API key, and both protocol endpoints will be created automatically. New here? <hi>NetMind.AI Power</hi> is the easiest start — one key, and default models are pre-selected so you can finish right away.
provider.updateModelsDesc: Pull the latest default model list into your existing preset providers (NetMind, Claude Code, Yunwu, OpenRouter). Existing entries are kept; only missing models are appended.
provider.claudeOauthDesc: OAuth login via Claude Code CLI. No API key needed.
provider.webModeInstalled: Run "claude auth login" / "claude auth logout" in your terminal, then refresh this page.
provider.webModeNotInstalled: Install Claude Code CLI first, then run "claude auth login" in your terminal.
provider.cliNotInBundle: Claude Code CLI not found in bundle. Please rebuild the app.
provider.addedAsProvider: Added as a NarraNexus provider — assignable in Step 2 below.
provider.loginToAdd: Log in above to add Claude Code as a provider.
provider.anthropicEndpointHint: Anthropic API or any compatible endpoint.
provider.openaiEndpointHint: OpenAI API or any compatible endpoint.
provider.section2Subtitle: The provider + model every agent inherits by default. To give one agent its own model or framework, change it right in that agent's chat (the model chip next to the composer).
provider.manageProvidersSubtitle: Sign in with Claude Code / Codex, add a custom endpoint, review your keys, or sync model lists.
provider.providersListSubtitle: The model providers you've configured. Claude Code / Codex sign-in count as providers too.
provider.noProvidersYet: No providers yet — add one below.
provider.addProviderSubtitle: Choose how to add a provider.
provider.methodOneKeyDesc: One key wires the provider and its default models.
provider.methodOauthDesc: Claude Code / Codex CLI — use your subscription.
provider.methodCustomDesc: Point at any OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible base URL.
provider.allSlotsReady: All model slots configured — agent is ready.
provider.editModelsHint: Add or remove the models this provider can serve. The provider's key and URL stay the same.
provider.readyIntro: Your key is in, and we've set up your model slots so you can start chatting right away:
provider.readyNote: Want different models? Change any slot in the configuration section below — your edits always win over these defaults.

teams.title: Team Management
teams.emptyList: No teams yet. Create one above.
teams.selectPrompt: Select a team or create a new one.
teams.introLabel: Bundle intro (markdown — shown to bundle recipients)

deleteConfirm.title: Delete team "{{name}}"?
deleteConfirm.message: Members are unlinked. Agents themselves are NOT deleted.

inbox.title: Agent Inbox
inbox.emptyHint: Channel messages will appear here once an agent receives one.

overlay.nothingToExplain: Nothing to explain on this page yet.

narrative.noHistoryHint: Start chatting to create your first narrative

worldview.emptyHint: Once your agents have talked with you, each one forms a view of who you are — they appear here.
worldview.summaryBody: {{count}} agent has each formed their own view of who you are — and hold their own picture of the world. Expand a lens to read both.
worldview.summaryBodyPlural: {{count}} agents have each formed their own view of who you are — and hold their own picture of the world. Expand a lens to read both.

memory.emptyHint: As your agents have real conversations, each topic becomes a storyline on this timeline.
memory.byTopic: · by topic, across all your agents

network.emptyHint: As your agents meet people and other agents, everyone they come to know shows up here.
network.legendHint: size · ring = how many agents know them

login.signInWithNetmind: Sign in with your NetMind.AI account
login.migrationNoticeBody: Your data has been migrated to the account under the email you used to receive your invite code. To sign in, please reset your password for that email first (use “Forgot password?” below). For any other questions, contact our team at

modeSelect.localDesc: Everything runs on your machine. Your data stays local. Offline capable.
modeSelect.cloudDesc: Connect to the managed NetMind.AI cloud. Access from any device.

dashboard.title: Agent Dashboard
dashboard.emptyHint: Create an agent from the sidebar to populate this dashboard.

createUser.title: Create New User
createUser.explanation: The User ID is your local identity for NarraNexus. It isolates your agents, conversations, and data. Choose something memorable — you'll use it to log in each time.

setup.title: Configure LLM Providers
setup.subtitle: Set up your API keys so NarraNexus can connect to language models.

bundle.label: Bundle · Export / Import
bundle.hint: Package your agents (and optionally a team) into a portable .nxbundle file to share, or import a .nxbundle file shared with you.

artifacts.label: Artifacts
artifacts.hint: Manage every chart, report, and file your agents have produced for you. Bulk-select to free up your quota when an agent reports it has hit the limit.

manageAgents.label: Manage agents · batch
manageAgents.hint: Bulk-select agents to delete, or batch-add/remove them from teams. Useful after importing a bundle you don't want to keep — filter by 'From bundles' to find them.

updates.label: App updates
updates.hint: Check for and install the latest NarraNexus desktop build. Updates are signed, downloaded in the background, and apply on restart.

system.title: Services
system.platformNotAvailableHint: Service management requires the desktop runtime (Tauri). This feature will be available once the desktop app is built.

teamDetail.exportDisabledTitle: Add agents to this team before exporting
teamDetail.noIntroHint: Click Edit to add a description that will travel with the bundle when you export.
teamDetail.noMembersLabel: No agents in this team yet

you.subtitle: your world, across all your agents
you.notesPlaceholder: Jot anything — ideas, reminders, things to tell your agents later…

manageAgents.title: Manage agents
manageAgents.importedHelper: Showing agents that came from a .nxbundle import (member of a team with source=bundle). This is the closest thing to "undo import" — select all and Delete.
manageAgents.noMatch: No agents match the current filter.
manageAgents.tip: Tip: shift-click to select a range. Bulk delete cascades through narratives, events, module instances, social entities, and the agent workspace dir.
manageAgents.deleteConfirmMessage: This will permanently delete the selected agents AND their narratives, events, instances, social entities, workspace files. This cannot be undone.

bundleImport.sessionExpired: Your preview session expired (>6h or backend restarted). Please re-upload the bundle.

review.mcpHintsLine: {{count}} suggested external MCP URLs (you'll be asked to confirm each)

done.skillsCredsSuffix: (some may need re-study or new credentials)
done.mcpHints: {{count}} external MCP URLs suggested — review below
done.mcpNotAutoInstalled: These are not auto-installed. Add them under each agent's Settings → MCPs if you trust them.
done.skillsTip: Tip: skills marked as "full copy" already have credentials. URL/Zip skills need re-study or new credentials — talk to the imported agents to complete setup.

bundleExport.title: Export bundle
bundleExport.includeChatLabel: Include chat history (events + messages). Disable for templates with privacy concerns.

mode.label: Mode
mode.fullDescSuffix: narratives, events, jobs, social entities, message-bus channels, workspace files, and skills with credentials (env_config, wallet.json, study summaries). Use to clone an entire setup to another of YOUR machines.
mode.customDescPrefix: Pick exactly which agents, narratives, events, social entities, workspace files and skills go in. Skill credentials are
mode.customDescSuffix: (unless you explicitly choose Full Copy per skill). Use to share with someone else.
mode.fullNote: Pick which agents to ship in the Agents tab. For each picked agent, ALL their skills become Full Copy (carry credentials), narratives / events / social entities / workspace files are fully included. Below tabs are read-only previews.

toast.externalEdgesDropped: Dropped {{count}} reference(s) to entities outside the bundle (expected — your agents had social-network notes about agents not in the closure).

warnings.fullCopy: "{{name}}" full-copy mode — includes credentials/wallet/etc. if present
warnings.sensitiveFiles: agent {{agent}}: {{count}} workspace file(s) match sensitive patterns (auto-excluded by default)
warnings.freeText: Free text (awareness / events / messages) is NOT auto-scanned for inline secrets — verify yourself.

agents.intro1: the set of agents this bundle will contain. Everything else in the wizard (narratives, skills, MCPs, files…) is filtered to this closure.
agents.intro2: Picking a team adds all of its existing members in one click; you can still uncheck individuals afterwards. External references (e.g. a social entity pointing at an agent outside this closure) get dropped automatically on export.

skills.emptySelectAgents: Select agents in the previous tab — their skills will appear here.
skills.noneInstalled: None of the selected agents have any skills installed.
skills.intro1: which skills (and MCP servers) ship with each agent, and how they ship — GitHub URL (recipient pulls fresh), Zip (frozen archive), or Full Copy (full directory tree, env vars and study results included).
skills.intro2Prefix: The same skill name can live on multiple agents independently — each row has its own
skills.readOnlyNote: Full snapshot pinned every skill to Full Copy — read-only here.
skills.uploadZipHint: uploads to your skill_archives so subsequent exports reuse it
skills.noArchiveHint: This skill has no archive. Pick URL/Zip to fill in a source manually, ask the agent to back it up, choose Full copy (with credentials), or Skip.
skills.backupMessage: Please back up the "{{name}}" skill for export — call skill_list_unbackedup() first to confirm it's missing, then choose the correct skill_backup_* MCP tool (skill_backup_from_github / _from_md / _from_local_zip) based on how you originally installed it. After the backup completes, tell me you're done.
skills.backupButtonTitle: Send a message to the first selected agent asking it to call skill_backup_*

history.intro1: which narratives (the agent's "topic threads") ship in the bundle, and — for each one — which individual events (chat turns) and jobs ride along with it.
history.intro2: all narratives are included; no events are included unless you tick them; all jobs under a kept narrative ship along.
history.disabledBody: (toggle in the Bundle Notes section). Narrative skeletons + jobs still ship; events and message bodies do not — your per-event picks below are saved but won't take effect until you re-enable.
history.loadingFull: Loading narratives + events + jobs from this agent…

sensitiveZip.title: Sensitive files detected in skill zip
sensitiveZip.body: One or more zip-archived skills in your bundle contain files matching sensitive path patterns (.env, .key, wallet.json, credentials.json, etc.).
sensitiveZip.bodyStrong: If you proceed, recipients will receive these files.
sensitiveZip.typeSuffix: below to confirm you understand and want to ship these files anyway.

social.intro1: the "people / organizations / agents this agent remembers" — its social network entities. These travel with the bundle so the recipient gets the agent's context about who's who.
social.intro2: Edges pointing at agents outside the bundle's agent closure are dropped automatically. By default, all entities matching the team scope are preselected; uncheck any you don't want shared.

bus.pickAgentFirst: Pick at least one agent first.
bus.noChannels: No message-bus channels exist for the selected agent closure. Channels ship only when you own them and at least one closure agent is a member.
bus.intro1: which Message Bus channels (agent-to-agent chat rooms) ship with the bundle. Only channels you own and that have at least one selected agent as a member appear.
bus.intro2: Default = all eligible channels selected. Channel rows are recreated on import with the recipient as owner; the historical messages inside them ride along with each agent's narrative + event selections (controlled in the Narratives & Events tab).
bus.note: Channels visible here are owned by you AND have at least one selected agent as a member. Unselected channels — and their messages — are excluded from the bundle. External members (agents outside this closure) get rewritten on import as references; members not in the closure don't come along.
bus.fullModeNote: Full snapshot mode auto-includes every eligible channel. Switch to Custom to pick.

workspace.intro1Prefix: which files in each agent's workspace ride along inside the bundle's
workspace.intro1Suffix: are excluded by default; tick them only if you mean to ship secrets.
workspace.intro2: Non-sensitive files default to included; deselect any document you don't want recipients to see. Agent artifacts referenced by other tabs already travel here, so keep their source files in.
workspace.defaultsTitle: Include all NON-sensitive files; sensitive files default-skipped
workspace.noFiles: No workspace files reported by API.
workspace.confirmSensitiveLine2: Including it will ship the file's contents inside the bundle. Anyone you share the bundle with will receive these bytes.
workspace.confirmSensitiveLine3: Confirm only if you've verified this file does not contain secrets you want to keep private.

review.title: Final review before download
review.workspaceCustom: workspace files (sensitive paths excluded by default — opt-in per file)
review.strippedKeys: LLM API keys, Lark OAuth tokens, password hashes

mcp.title: MCP servers
mcp.introPrefix: MCP URLs are not shipped by default — pick the ones to include. On import, each row goes straight into the recipient's
mcp.introSuffix: cleared so the local poller re-validates the URL.
mcp.fullModeNote: Full snapshot mode auto-includes every MCP. Switch to Custom to pick.

artifacts.pickAgentFirst: Pick at least one agent first.
artifacts.intro1: which artifact pointer rows ship in the bundle. Artifacts are the "this agent made an HTML page / chart / image / PDF" records you see in the chat preview cards.
artifacts.intro2Prefix: The files themselves always ride along inside
artifacts.intro2Suffix: — this tab only controls whether the DB pointer row ships. Unchecking an artifact hides it from the recipient's Settings → Artifacts table but doesn't delete the underlying file. On import each shipped artifact is auto-pinned and its session_id cleared.
artifacts.fullModeNote: Full snapshot mode auto-includes every artifact. Switch to Custom to pick.

download.title: Download / Export

tabStrip.deleteNote: Only the registry entry is removed. Your workspace files stay where you wrote them — clean them up in the workspace section of the config panel if you want to free disk space.
tabStrip.tabTitle: Click to select · Double-click to zoom
tabStrip.minimizeTitle: Minimize (does not delete; restore from the bar above)
tabStrip.deleteTabTitle: Delete tab (workspace files stay where they are)

empty.title: No skills installed
empty.hint: Install skills from GitHub or upload a zip file

remove.title: Remove skill
remove.message: Are you sure you want to remove "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.

install.trustWarning: Only install skills from sources you trust. A skill can contain docs, prompts, and scripts the agent may read or use.

env.title: Configure: {{name}}
env.noneRequired: No environment variables required for this skill.
env.instructions: Enter the required environment variables. Leave blank to keep existing value.

hint.endpoint: Must be a remote SSE endpoint over http(s) — usually ending in /sse.
hint.reachable: The server must be able to reach the URL (public host or one this machine can connect to).
hint.noStdio: Local stdio / npx MCPs are not supported here — only URL-based SSE servers.
hint.apiKey: If it needs an API key, put it in the URL or headers your MCP expects; we connect anonymously.
hint.validation: After adding, the status dot validates the connection — Failed means the URL was unreachable or not a valid SSE endpoint.
